title: rosa canina subvar. sarmentoides
intro: Rosa canina subvar. sarmentoides, also known as wild rose or dog rose, is a climbing shrub that belongs to the family Rosaceae. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its hips, which are high in vitamins A and C, flavonoids, and other antioxidants, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The hips of Rosa canina subvar. sarmentoides have been used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. Rosa canina subvar. sarmentoides can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
